reasoning: According to multiple recent authoritative sources from late 2025, SpaceX has only conducted five Starship launches in 2025 as of November. The stated criteria requires between 10 and 12 Starship launches to reach an altitude of 62 miles in 2025. Given that only 5 launches have taken place by November 2025, it is physically impossible for the total number of launches reaching 62 miles to suddenly reach 10-12 by December 31, 2025. This extremely low launch rate is consistent with the Polymarket price of 1% that was observed on November 27, 2025. Therefore, I estimate the probability of 10-12 successful launches reaching space in 2025 to be at the minimum allowed bound (0.02).
